summary: Complete testing of Cases page (Kanban board) for AMLGuard. 24/24 backend tests passed. All frontend UI flows working correctly including Kanban board with 4 columns, stats bar, case cards with urgency dots, case detail panel with assignment dropdown, SAR report generation, and activity log with comments.

issues: Minor console warning: 'In HTML, <span> cannot be a child of <option>' - React hydration warning in dropdown options, does not affect functionality

passed_tests: Backend: GET /api/cases returns list of 14 cases
Backend: Cases have required fields (id, case_id, customer_name, case_type, priority, status, created_at)
Backend: All cases have valid statuses (open, in_progress, escalated, closed)
Backend: All cases have valid priorities (critical, high, medium, low)
Backend: All cases have valid case types (pep_match, sanctions_match, adverse_media, suspicious_transaction)
Backend: GET /api/cases/stats returns status counts (total, open, in_progress, escalated, closed, sar_filed)
Backend: Stats total matches sum of status counts
Backend: GET /api/team-members returns 3 team members
Backend: Team members have correct data (Priya Sharma - Compliance Officer, Rahul Verma - Senior Analyst, Anita Desai - MLRO)
Backend: PATCH /api/cases/{id}/status updates case status
Backend: PATCH /api/cases/{id}/status rejects invalid status with 400
Backend: PATCH /api/cases/{id}/status returns 404 for nonexistent case
Backend: POST /api/cases/{id}/resolve accepts valid resolution types
Backend: POST /api/cases/{id}/resolve rejects invalid resolution type with 400
Backend: PUT /api/cases/{id}/assign assigns case to team member
Backend: PUT /api/cases/{id}/assign with null unassigns case
Backend: POST /api/cases/{id}/generate-sar returns mock SAR report with pre-filled data
Backend: POST /api/cases/{id}/generate-sar returns 404 for nonexistent case
Backend: GET /api/cases/{id}/notes returns notes list
Backend: POST /api/cases/{id}/notes adds comment to case
Backend: Case notes have required fields (id, case_id, note, created_at)
Backend: 8+ demo cases seeded on startup
Backend: Demo cases have created_at for urgency calculation
Backend: Demo cases distributed across statuses
Frontend: Login with shyam@sentrixai.com / Sentrix@2024 works
Frontend: Cases page shows 'Case Management' title
Frontend: Stats bar displays Total Cases (14), Open Alerts (6), Escalated (2), SAR Filed (4)
Frontend: Kanban board renders with 4 columns (New Alerts, Under Investigation, Escalated, Resolved)
Frontend: 14 case cards displayed on the board
Frontend: Case cards show case ID, customer name, case type icon, priority badge
Frontend: 14 urgency dots visible on cards (colored based on age)
Frontend: SAR badges visible on some cards
Frontend: 5 cards show assigned users (Priya, Rahul, Anita)
Frontend: Clicking case card opens slide-out detail panel
Frontend: Detail panel shows case ID, priority badge, customer name, status, case type
Frontend: Detail panel shows description section
Frontend: Detail panel shows Case Information (Risk Score, Risk Level, Created, Due Date)
Frontend: Detail panel has Assign To dropdown with 3 team members + Unassigned option
Frontend: Detail panel has Generate SAR Report button
Frontend: Generate SAR Report creates mock SAR with pre-filled customer data
Frontend: SAR Report displays reference, subject info, risk assessment, narrative
Frontend: Detail panel has Activity Log with comment input
Frontend: Adding comment persists and displays in activity log
Frontend: Panel closes via X button
Frontend: Panel closes via overlay click
Frontend: Assignment change via dropdown works
Frontend: Column counts match stats bar (6+4+2+2=14)
Frontend: Dark theme confirmed (rgb(8, 12, 18) background)
critical_code_review_comments: Minor: React hydration warning in CaseDetailPanel dropdown - <span> inside <option> element. Consider using plain text instead of dynamic spans in select options.
seed_data_creation: 8 demo cases seeded on startup linked to demo customers. Cases include: Rajendra Prasad Yadav (PEP Match, critical), Balakrishnan Nair Pillai (Sanctions Match, critical), Smt. Laxmi Devi Sharma (PEP Match, high), Kabir Singhania (Adverse Media, medium), Manish Tiwari (Suspicious Transaction, high), Siddharth Malhotra (Sanctions Match, high), Rohit Choudhary (Adverse Media, medium, closed with SAR), Harish Pandey (Suspicious Transaction, medium).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Cases page (Kanban board) fully tested. Features verified: Kanban board with 4 columns (New Alerts=open, Under Investigation=in_progress, Escalated=escalated, Resolved=closed), stats bar (Total Cases, Open Alerts, Escalated, SAR Filed), case cards with urgency dots (red >7 days, yellow 3-7 days, green <3 days), priority badges, case type icons, SAR badges, assigned user. Case detail slide-out panel with description, risk info, Assign To dropdown (3 team members), Generate SAR Report button (mock SAR with pre-filled data), activity log with comments. Resolution modal appears when dragging to Resolved column (not tested via DnD but backend endpoint verified). Backend endpoints: GET /api/cases, GET /api/cases/stats, GET /api/team-members, PATCH /api/cases/{id}/status, POST /api/cases/{id}/resolve, PUT /api/cases/{id}/assign, POST /api/cases/{id}/generate-sar, GET /api/cases/{id}/notes, POST /api/cases/{id}/notes. Signzy KYC and OpenSanctions AML screening APIs are MOCKED. SAR Report Generation is MOCKED.

mocked_apis.SAR Report Generation: POST /api/cases/{id}/generate-sar returns mock SAR report with pre-filled customer data

kanban_board.drag_and_drop: Uses @dnd-kit/core - backend endpoint verified, DnD UI not tested

case_cards.sar_badges: Red SAR badge on cards with sar_filed=true
case_cards.assigned_user: Shows first name of assigned team member

case_detail_panel.generate_sar_button: Visible when case not closed and SAR not filed
activity_log: Notes list with author and timestamp
